fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: High-growth company with impressive revenue and earnings expansion, but trades at a very high valuation and has thin operating margins.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong top and bottom-line expansion.
Gross margin of 79.9% is best-in-class, supporting high-margin services.
Free cash flow of $936.73M TTM demonstrates solid cash generation.
Consensus rating is strong buy with a mean target of $223.93, suggesting analyst optimism.
Current ratio of 3.40 and quick ratio of 3.29 indicate excellent short-term liquidity.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 580.39 and forward P/E of 77.49 are extremely high, indicating a very expensive stock.
Operating margin of 0.8% and profit margin of 3.7% are thin despite high gross margins, suggesting high operating expenses.
Return on assets of -0.2% is negative, indicating inefficient use of assets.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22 is high, though offset by substantial cash reserves.

news.paragraph: Datadog is positioned to benefit as AI applications move into production, with recent analyst reports highlighting its strong position and a raised price target to $255. The company recently surpassed $1 billion in quarterly revenue and provided a higher outlook for 2026, signaling robust growth. A golden cross technical signal also suggests positive momentum, with analysts maintaining a strong buy consensus.
